hypothesis: If machine learning interatomic potentials (EquiformerV2 and eSEN) are used to pre-converge solid-state nudged elastic band pathways before DFT refinement, then the hybrid ML-SSNEB framework will significantly reduce the total number of expensive DFT SSNEB iterations required to achieve convergence compared to pure DFT-based SSNEB, because the ML models provide sufficiently accurate initial pathway geometries that place the system closer to the true minimum energy pathway, thereby reducing the computational burden of the DFT refinement stage.
